title: Asukas Phone Conversation With Misato
wikitext:
[]: Asuka's Phone Conversation With Misato             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:04 pm Split from "Is Asuka Overrated as a Pilot?" thread. It seems in this forum exists a general consensus, that "rebuild Asuka" relinquished her interests in Shinji in favor of Rei. This seems to be founded from the phone conversation with Misato, prior to 03's rampage. Now I've only watched the scene twice and only the german dub, but I see no indication whatsoever for this conclussion, all she talks about is wanting to be more connected, to have friends... It can't just be the fact that she forego's visiting the dinner party, can it? Because to me this was more motivated by her wanting to pilot an EVA. Is the german dubbing way off and she actually says something different, or am I missing something? I'd really appreciate it, if someone could give me a short explanation,could very well be in pm, because I don't want to push the thread totally off-topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:14 pm die-yng wrote: On a totally different topic: !!!!! It seems in this forum exists a general consensus, that "rebuild Asuka" relinquished her interests in Shinji in favor of Rei. This seems to be founded from the phone conversation with Misato, prior to 03's rampage. Now I've only watched the scene twice and only the german dub, but I see no indication whatsoever for this conclussion, all she talks about is wanting to be more connected, to have friends... It can't just be the fact that she forego's visiting the dinner party, can it? Because to me this was more motivated by her wanting to pilot an EVA. Is the german dubbing way off and she actually says something different, or am I missing something? I'd really appreciate it, if someone could give me a short explanation,could very well be in pm, because I don't want to push the thread totally off-topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:40 pm die-yng wrote: Thanks, just read through the post and I guess it is a legitimate interpretation. Stiil, it could be read differently as well, yes, she choses the EVA instead of the invitation, so piloting is the most important thing to her. But the elevator scene could be interpreted as her noticing that Rei's not so different from her after all and she was just trying to be nice to her for once. That doesn't neccessarily mean she's totally given up on Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Dec 29, 2010 9:19 pm Yes, that's how I would read it as well. Of course it could be read as her giving up on Shinji's love, but it could as well mean the above. In fact any interpretation depends on what role Asuka is suppossed to play in rebuild. If it is basically the same as in NGE, then the first interpretation would make sense. If they want to remove her as love interest for Shinji, than the later reading makes sense. I guess we will know for sure when 3.0 finally arrives.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 29, 2010 9:55 pm The situantion pretty much was one girl gets the boy, the other gets the robot. The EVA 03 activation test was on the Date of Rei's dinner party. EVA 00 is being Repaired, EVA 02 is frozen. Rei decides to be nice and tells Asuka that she can be happy without EVA as EVA is just a 'mirror of her own heart'. Catfight ensues, except this time Rei halts the slap - and Asuka notices that Rei has much more bandages on her fingers than he has (they'be both been trying to cook stuff for Shinji which Misato called "both of them showing intrest in boys" - except Rei wanted to get the Ikaris to recincile as well. Via Dinner Party. ) Asuka asks Rei what she feels for the baka. Rei says something akin to "warm and fuzzy". Asuka walks away, calling her an ultra baka and realizing that this is luv. (She explicitly says that) Asuka chose the robot and showed a small smile when she got thanked by the lucky fellow that got the boy. Misato suspects that Asuka has become N-I-C-E. Asuka says she just wanted the robot. cut to later - Asuka phones Misato and starts telling her that going to that party and pretending to be happy would have worn her out after all, and that she's fine "protecting the ignorant masses" in EVA. and how she was always fine on her own anyway. So far, it sounds like the good old Asuka (except her voice is very happy/relaxxed during all of this). but then she remarks that as a generall thing, she thinks it's wothwhile to be around others and that talking to Misato has made her feel better, but that she still doesn't think she fits in here. Misato tells her that she isa wrong and that she has become a kind hearted person. When she gets into the plug, Asuka is surprised that she is actually able to smile honestly - and just as the audience was going aww, destiny decided to be a bitch. Hello Bardiel. Shinji isn't mentioned in the phone call dialogue, but if you look at it in context...

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Dec 29, 2010 10:18 pm Kendrix wrote: The situantion pretty much was one girl gets the boy, the other gets the robot. The EVA 03 activation test was on the Date of Rei's dinner party. EVA 00 is being Repaired, EVA 02 is frozen. Rei decides to be nice and tells Asuka that she can be happy without EVA as EVA is just a 'mirror of her own heart'. Catfight ensues, except this time Rei halts the slap - and Asuka notices that Rei has much more bandages on her fingers than he has (they'be both been trying to cook stuff for Shinji which Misato called "both of them showing intrest in boys" - except Rei wanted to get the Ikaris to recincile as well. Via Dinner Party. ) Asuka asks Rei what she feels for the baka. Rei says something akin to "warm and fuzzy". Asuka walks away, calling her an ultra baka and realizing that this is luv. (She explicitly says that) Asuka chose the robot and showed a small smile when she got thanked by the lucky fellow that got the boy. Misato suspects that Asuka has become N-I-C-E. Asuka says she just wanted the robot. cut to later - Asuka phones Misato and starts telling her that going to that party and pretending to be happy would have worn her out after all, and that she's fine "protecting the ignorant masses" in EVA. and how she was always fine on her own anyway. So far, it sounds like the good old Asuka (except her voice is very happy/relaxxed during all of this). but then she remarks that as a generall thing, she thinks it's wothwhile to be around others and that talking to Misato has made her feel better, but that she still doesn't think she fits in here. Misato tells her that she isa wrong and that she has become a kind hearted person. When she gets into the plug, Asuka is surprised that she is actually able to smile honestly - and just as the audience was going aww, destiny decided to be a bitch. Hello Bardiel. Shinji isn't mentioned in the phone call dialogue, but if you look at it in context...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 29, 2010 11:00 pm die-yng wrote: True, true, especially considering the usual Japanese understatement. But still, there is room left for interpretation. It might be wishful thinking on my part, because I'm still wishing that Asuka returns full throttle in 3.0. But I agree with you that this is the most likeable way to view the situation, even if it's not my favorite.

[]:             Quote Post by Mad » Thu Dec 30, 2010 3:59 am I think we should take time to look at Rei's motivation for the dinner party as well. Let's go back to the scene with Gendo and Rei having lunch; Gendo obviously sees the Yui starting to come out in Rei, he even smiles towards Rei's motherly actions. And I think Rei is acting just like that, a mother or perhaps a sister towards Shinji. He is the first person after all to have showed kindness to her without later sticking a needle in her arm and telling her to pilot a giant Bio-Mech. So I think that Shinji managed to spark the motherly instinct in Rei and now she feels a need to mend the relationship between the two most important people in her life. Asuka then misinterprets Rei's feelings and actions as romantic relationship instead of a mother-son (brother-sister?) relationship. Asuka then goes back to her old ways of pushing people away to save herself from emotional damage, hops in the Eva, and gets Angelfied. Or it's 3 o'clock in the morning and I'm tired as hell and just wanted to post something after logging in for the first time in over a year.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Dec 30, 2010 8:37 am @Sachi: There was this thread ([url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=10397[/url]), but it got watered down and was locked. @Mad: Check out the "Shinji/Rei in 3.0/Q" thread. Shamelessly plugging myself, start reading from here (near the end): [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?p=411226#411226[/url]. --- I removed the long post I had put here, and gave it its own topic, because it did not relate to the Asuka-Misato telephone conversation in the end. More shameless plugging to this new topic: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=10460[/url] Thank you for bearing with me.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 31, 2010 5:42 pm die-yng wrote: What I think is funny, is that a lot of people give the start of the conversation so much importance. The part where she talks about the elite having to do something for the masses. I've read here and there that this shows how conceited and arrogant Asuka still is, while to me it clearly was just an attempt to make her decision seem more like a flight than honest sympathy. Misato through her answers shows that she knows, that Asuka's regular behaviour is just a facade for the public. Since Rebuild Misato seems to know her pretty well, it would be pretty useless to do her regular act for Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Sat Jan 01, 2011 11:51 am As far as Rebuild goes, I'm agreeing with you 100%. Nothing to discuss. As for NGE, to me there were enough instances, where Asuka's inner torment broke through her act. I remember (I was younger) that I was really annoyed about Shinji's failure to help her in any way. Was she pushing him away? Yes. But he could have made more of an effort to actually get through to her. I won't even try to defend A. Sohryu's treatment of Baka Shinji, it is so unbelievable cruel sometimes, you really can't defend it. But I understand it. Shinji is so timid and non-aggressive, always willing to back down, almost inviting others to bully him and Asuka does with an almost religious zeal. I guess a part of it could also be contributed to hurting the one you love/ like. Asuka is almost tptally unable to enter an honest rapport with others, so I always theorized that her bullying was in a way an attempt to connect with Shinji in the only way it was possible for her. Whenever her real feelings/ her inner self shone through. she had to find a way to hide it. So she went : BAM!! Baka Shinji! or "Now I have to wash my mouth.." BTW happy new year to everyone!!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 01, 2011 12:49 pm What I wanted to say was not Asuka's feelings didn't break through in old!NGE (They did - most prominently in ep. 16 (She appeared at the hospital after having badmouthed him so much that even Rei got angry - Aparently, she WAS worried after all. Can't deny it was cute.) and 22 ("Why am I even talking to you?!") the point is that she didn't allow herself that/tried to hide them. I was focusing on Asuka's part here, but yeah, it's the combination that makes their relationship so difficult - Shinji is at least well-intentioned, honest, and thinks about how Asuka(and everyone else) must be feeling, but it's hard for him to act on these thoughts/feelings, because he's afraid of doing it wrong. (Not only with Asuka - He couldn't bring himself to do anything against Misato crying her eyes out or tell Kaworu that he liked/loved him as well) and Asuka is the type that needs affirmation/aknowdledgement like she needs air. And after the story of her parents(Her father screwing another woman within earshot of her and her mother - Man, Mr. Langley, at least get a room!), it must be pretty hard for her not to know whether she has him all for herself. Indecisiveness IS one of Shinji's weaknesses

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Sat Jan 01, 2011 2:08 pm Kendrix wrote: Indecisiveness IS one of Shinji's weaknesses

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Sat Jan 01, 2011 2:55 pm die-yng wrote: That is so true. Sadly in the end when it counts, he isn't able to help any of the people, who are close to him. This bugs me a little bit, because it's not like he never tried to help anybody, after all he was the one who reached out to Rei. He just stops at one point, and then it becomes really painful to watch, to me it still is, after all this years. I always want to gram his shoulders and yell at him, to do something to help. Do you think it falls together with Rei II's death or more with him having to kill Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Sun Jan 02, 2011 12:35 pm I'm not quite sure I'd agree with you, since I meant the point when Shinji didn't do anything to help others with their problems (not fighting evas) and to me that happened earlier in the line of events. Still it makes no sense to start a discussion over this here, maybe I'll start a thread in the other section.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 02, 2011 9:30 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: This should probably not be in the Rebuild section of the forum, but I'll answer your question. ---- Shinji learned to reach out to people over the course of the series. He made friends with Misato, Toji, Rei and Kaji. He was attracted to Asuka. He even got a few words of praise from his father. And Kaworu made him feel totally accepted. And then it all went down the tube. His father forced his/Unit-01's hands to perform a beastly act he would not voluntarily do. Toji got maimed for life as a direct result. Kaji got himself killed (how dare he?!). Misato withdrew from him because after Kaji showed up (and even more after he died). Rei first got killed, then came back, forgot she was his friend and ended up being something too creepy for words. Asuka ended up in a coma. And he was betrayed by Kaworu, and forced to kill him at his own request. None of this was Shinji's fault. And when he had nobody else left, he went back to Asuka and did something so shameful he thought he could never face her again. This was his fault. So he started to believe it must have all been his fault. The more people he lost, the more he distanced himself from the rest of humanity. In the end, he completely stopped trying, and Misato had to literally drag him forward. Shinji's conviction at that point was that it was better not to care for others at all, so that he could no longer get hurt. It was all that Misato could do to get him to try one last time, to help Asuka, only to find out that because of his earlier indecisiveness she had been torn to shreds already. And along comes a vision of Kaworu to promise him an end to the pain. Human Instrumentality begins.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Mon Jan 03, 2011 9:41 am Eva 04 wrote: *Ahem*. This might be off topic but regarding the Unit 03 accident,that was Shinji's fault. It was stated in the manga it was. He decided to not fight(which could have saved Toji instead of him losing a leg and dying in the manga) and let his father activate the dummy system which lead to complete and total Annihilation of Unit 03. People seem to forget that what happened with that accident is his fault. It was,as I said,pretty much outright stated in the manga after it happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jan 03, 2011 10:32 am Eva 04 wrote: *Ahem*. This might be off topic but regarding the Unit 03 accident,that was Shinji's fault. It was stated in the manga it was. He decided to not fight(which could have saved Toji instead of him losing a leg and dying in the manga) and let his father activate the dummy system which lead to complete and total Annihilation of Unit 03. People seem to forget that what happened with that accident is his fault. It was,as I said,pretty much outright stated in the manga after it happened.

Article: Asukas Phone Conversation With Misato
[]: Asuka's Phone Conversation With Misato             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:04 pm Split from "Is Asuka Overrated as a Pilot?" thread. It seems in this forum exists a general consensus, that "rebuild Asuka" relinquished her interests in Shinji in favor of Rei. This seems to be founded from the phone conversation with Misato, prior to 03's rampage. Now I've only watched the scene twice and only the german dub, but I see no indication whatsoever for this conclussion, all she talks about is wanting to be more connected, to have friends... It can't just be the fact that she forego's visiting the dinner party, can it? Because to me this was more motivated by her wanting to pilot an EVA. Is the german dubbing way off and she actually says something different, or am I missing something? I'd really appreciate it, if someone could give me a short explanation,could very well be in pm, because I don't want to push the thread totally off-topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:14 pm die-yng wrote: On a totally different topic: !!!!! It seems in this forum exists a general consensus, that "rebuild Asuka" relinquished her interests in Shinji in favor of Rei. This seems to be founded from the phone conversation with Misato, prior to 03's rampage. Now I've only watched the scene twice and only the german dub, but I see no indication whatsoever for this conclussion, all she talks about is wanting to be more connected, to have friends... It can't just be the fact that she forego's visiting the dinner party, can it? Because to me this was more motivated by her wanting to pilot an EVA. Is the german dubbing way off and she actually says something different, or am I missing something? I'd really appreciate it, if someone could give me a short explanation,could very well be in pm, because I don't want to push the thread totally off-topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:40 pm die-yng wrote: Thanks, just read through the post and I guess it is a legitimate interpretation. Stiil, it could be read differently as well, yes, she choses

Article: Asukas Phone Conversation With Misato
 Wed Dec 29, 2010 8:40 pm die-yng wrote: Thanks, just read through the post and I guess it is a legitimate interpretation. Stiil, it could be read differently as well, yes, she choses the EVA instead of the invitation, so piloting is the most important thing to her. But the elevator scene could be interpreted as her noticing that Rei's not so different from her after all and she was just trying to be nice to her for once. That doesn't neccessarily mean she's totally given up on Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Dec 29, 2010 9:19 pm Yes, that's how I would read it as well. Of course it could be read as her giving up on Shinji's love, but it could as well mean the above. In fact any interpretation depends on what role Asuka is suppossed to play in rebuild. If it is basically the same as in NGE, then the first interpretation would make sense. If they want to remove her as love interest for Shinji, than the later reading makes sense. I guess we will know for sure when 3.0 finally arrives.

Rei decides to be nice and tells Asuka that she can be happy without EVA as EVA is just a 'mirror of her own heart'. Catfight ensues, except this time Rei halts the slap - and Asuka notices that Rei has much more bandages on her fingers than he has (they'be both been trying to cook stuff for Shinji which Misato called "both of them showing intrest in boys" - except Rei wanted to get the Ikaris to recincile as well. Via Dinner Party. ) Asuka asks Rei what she feels for the baka. Rei says something akin to "warm and fuzzy". Asuka walks away, calling her an ultra baka and realizing that this is luv. (She explicitly says that) Asuka chose the robot and showed a small smile when she got thanked by the lucky fellow that got the boy. Mis

[]:             Quote Post by Mad » Thu Dec 30, 2010 3:59 am I think we should take time to look at Rei's motivation for the dinner party as well. Let's go back to the scene with Gendo and Rei having lunch; Gendo obviously sees the Yui starting to come out in Rei, he even smiles towards Rei's motherly actions. And I think Rei is acting just like that,

Article: Asukas Phone Conversation With Misato
. Let's go back to the scene with Gendo and Rei having lunch; Gendo obviously sees the Yui starting to come out in Rei, he even smiles towards Rei's motherly actions. And I think Rei is acting just like that, a mother or perhaps a sister towards Shinji. He is the first person after all to have showed kindness to her without later sticking a needle in her arm and telling her to pilot a giant Bio-Mech. So I think that Shinji managed to spark the motherly instinct in Rei and now she feels a need to mend the relationship between the two most important people in her life. Asuka then misinterprets Rei's feelings and actions as romantic relationship instead of a mother-son (brother-sister?) relationship. Asuka then goes back to her old ways of pushing people away to save herself from emotional damage, hops in the Eva, and gets Angelfied. Or it's 3 o'clock in the morning and I'm tired as hell and just wanted to post something after logging in for the first time in over a year.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sun Jan 02, 2011 9:30 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: This should probably not be in the Rebuild section of the forum, but I'll answer your question. ---- Shinji learned to reach out to people over the course of the series. He made friends with Misato, Toji, Rei and Kaji. He was attracted to Asuka. He even got a few words of praise from his father. And Kaworu made him feel totally accepted. And then it all went down the tube. His father forced his/Unit-01's hands to perform a beastly act he would not voluntarily do. Toji got maimed for life as a direct result. Kaji got himself killed (how dare he?!). Misato withdrew from him because after Kaji showed up (and even more after he died). Rei first got killed, then came back, forgot she was his friend and ended up being something too creepy for words. Asuka ended up in a coma. And he was betrayed by Kaworu, and forced to kill him at his own request. None of this was Shinji's fault. And when he had nobody else left, he went back to Asuka and did something so shameful he thought he could never face her again. This was his fault. So he started to believe it must have all been his fault. The more people he lost, the more he distanced himself from the rest of humanity. In the end, he completely stopped trying, and Misato had to literally drag

Article: Asukas Phone Conversation With Misato
 his fault. So he started to believe it must have all been his fault. The more people he lost, the more he distanced himself from the rest of humanity. In the end, he completely stopped trying, and Misato had to literally drag him forward. Shinji's conviction at that point was that it was better not to care for others at all, so that he could no longer get hurt. It was all that Misato could do to get him to try one last time, to help Asuka, only to find out that because of his earlier indecisiveness she had been torn to shreds already. And along comes a vision of Kaworu to promise him an end to the pain. Human Instrumentality begins.
